ai_strategy_lead.name: CTLA GenAI fellows / AI Think Tank
ai_strategy_lead.title: Public AI strategy or initiative lead / hub identified from cited sources

ai_strategy.summary: Ohio University has a public AI posture anchored in CTLA GenAI fellows / AI Think Tank. The cited sources show activity across responsible-use guidance, teaching resources, research or infrastructure, and AI literacy or workforce preparation.

key_provisions: Use AI transparently and follow course, research, workplace and academic-integrity expectations.
Do not enter confidential, regulated, sensitive, unpublished or protected institutional data into unapproved public AI tools.
Human users remain accountable for accuracy, bias, copyright, privacy and final decisions based on AI output.
ai_policy.summary: Ohio University publishes AI policy or guidance with a permissive stance: AI use is allowed or actively explored, but bounded by privacy, ethics, academic integrity, compliance and human-review expectations.

bodies.scope: Public AI strategy, guidance, teaching, research or technology coordination.

governance_structure.summary: AI governance at Ohio University is evidenced through CTLA GenAI fellows / AI Think Tank and the cited policy or strategy sources. Public sources did not identify a separate chief AI officer during this sweep.

initiatives.description: Ohio University's Center for Teaching, Learning, and Assessment supports faculty policies, course design, GenAI faculty fellows, learning communities, and AI-aware course redesign examples.

computing_infrastructure.supercomputer_name: Ohio Supercomputer Center Cardinal (state resource used in Ohio University project)
computing_infrastructure.summary: No Ohio University-owned AI GPU cluster or supercomputer count surfaced in public sources. Ohio University students did, however, collaborate with the Ohio Supercomputer Center on Cardinal, OSC's AI-capable HPC cluster, and campus researchers publish HPC work through Russ College.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Responsible and inclusive AI access

regulatory_context.summary: No binding state higher-education AI policy was identified during this sweep; institutional guidance is the main policy evidence.

narrative_summary: Ohio University has a documented AI posture across policy, teaching, research, infrastructure or access. The central public signal is CTLA GenAI fellows / AI Think Tank, supported by policy or guidance sources that encourage responsible use while emphasizing privacy, academic integrity, ethics and human oversight. The institution also shows curriculum or AI literacy activity and at least one research, center, infrastructure or applied innovation signal. Its maturity is strongest where public sources show durable structures: committees, centers, portals, degree programs, supercomputing, enterprise tools, or workforce initiatives. The gaps are also important: this sweep did not find a standalone chief AI officer in public sources for most assigned institutions, and several initiatives do not disclose exact budgets, user counts or implementation metrics. Overall, the evidence supports a medium-confidence R1 profile with enough public signals to compare the institution across governance, teaching, research, enterprise tools, workforce and equity dimensions.
